conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You mentioned you used to enjoy walking the dog—what else has started to feel like a chore lately?
Cooking. I look at the recipe and just feel like I can't do it. I end up ordering in just to get through the evening.
When you feel like you can't do something simple like cooking, what goes through your mind about yourself?
I think I’m losing myself. I used to be the one who could handle anything, and now I’m just tired and struggling.
When you think about the future, what comes to mind?
I don’t really think about it. I just take it one day at a time because the thought of planning for tomorrow is too much.
You mentioned taking it one day at a time—does that make you feel more on edge or irritable?
Yes, it does. I just feel like I’m constantly waiting for the energy to come back, but it never does.
When you say you're waiting for the energy to come back, what does that waiting feel like?
It feels like waiting for a miracle that isn't coming. I just sit there and stare at the wall, hoping something will change, but it doesn't.
When you're sitting there staring at the wall, what kind of thoughts tend to come up?
Mostly just how empty the house feels. I look around and think, 'This is it. This is what I've got left.'
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
After the divorce. It used to be just a quiet sadness, but now it’s more like... I don't know, a realization that I’m just existing, not really living.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. The days blend together, and I don’t have anyone to share it with.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to find the motivation to do stuff during the day?
Definitely. I just feel so drained. Even the simple things, like going to the gym, seem like too much effort.
You mentioned thinking about things you could have done differently—what kinds of things come to mind?
Just stuff from the past, like work or relationships. I keep thinking that if I had done things differently, maybe I wouldn't be in this situation now.
When you think about being in this situation now, what does that look like for you?
It looks like I failed. I keep thinking that I should be further along by now, and I’m just stuck here.
When you say you feel stuck, what does that feel like day to day?
It’s like I’m just going through the motions. I do things, but I don’t actually feel like I’m moving forward.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things will ever change for you?
I don’t know. I just can’t see it happening. I feel like I’m stuck in this loop, and I don't know how to get out of it.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
It’s been building up over the past few months. It just feels like the more time goes on, the more I realize I have nothing to show for it.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. I just don't see the point in doing anything anymore, because I don't believe I can ever be happy.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't see them unfolding at all. I just see me sitting here, waiting for something to happen that never does.
